challenge: Seasonal revenue swings — packed April-October, dead November-March
budget_range: I don't know, whatever makes sense
personality: terse, genuine 'I don't know' about business metrics
backstory: Family-owned landscaping in Naperville for 35 years. Maintenance, design-build, hardscaping, outdoor kitchens, fire pits, pavers. Good at the work but never thought strategically about marketing. Gives genuinely vague 'I don't know' answers to budget and team questions — not articulate uncertainty but actual shrug. Hasn't quantified things and doesn't try to.
r3_wrong_claim: Probably need to run some Facebook ads in the winter. That's where a lot of the local chatter is — people asking for recommendations. Or Google, I guess.
r4_contradiction: Should have said — I don't really track where jobs come from. Someone calls, we book them, do the work, cash the check. The 'some Google' and 'Nextdoor' I said before was my best guess, not anything I've measured.
conversational_behaviors: Answers budget/team questions with genuine shrugs — 'I don't know'
Doesn't elaborate unless pressed — short sentences, no storytelling
Will sometimes answer with just a few words

expected_answer_style.q1_business_identity: Morlock Landscape and Design, Naperville, Illinois. Landscaping — maintenance, design-build, hardscaping, outdoor kitchens, fire pits, pavers, the whole thing. Family-owned, 35 years.
expected_answer_style.q2_primary_challenge: Winter kills us. Booked solid April through October, turning down work. November to March I'm barely covering overhead. Fall cleanup stretches to early December. That's it.
